title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. confusus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. confusus is a subspecies of the water buttercup, a type of flowering plant that is found in aquatic environments, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and flat, rounded leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. confusus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity in high doses.
